title: Network tests: ping redhat.com, not podman.io
body:
Much as we'd love to eat our dogfood, podman.io is not hosted
on reliable infrastructure; redhat.com is. Let's see if this
gets rid of CI flakes.

Closes: #17044

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Since neither of these failures are related to the PR, force merging
LGTM

Oh... well, keep in mind that merging this isn't going to fix anything.  Per [Paul's comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17044#issuecomment-1377386360), the bug seems to be elsewhere. Sorry, I probably should've just closed this PR. Or, I see a `Revert` button, will that do what I hope it does?
I believe pinging redhat.com is better anyways for the statement. The issue we are dealing with now is a bad kernel.
